behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at module handoff boundaries (manifest validation -> loader decision -> import suppression). A unit test of only the validator can miss a loader bug that still imports/executes plugin code. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: it only mentions local plugin loading/manifest fields, with no real network/subprocess/external API (so not System), no AI output dependence (not Agentic), and no multi-agentic chain (not Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugin directory with one invalid manifest and an entry module that writes a sentinel file on import. Run the real loader over that directory. Assert the loader reports manifest rejection and the sentinel side effect never appears, proving no plugin code executed.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: Order correctness depends on resolver + loader + hook invocation wiring across multiple modules, not one local function. A pure unit test of sort logic can miss wiring/order bugs in initialization flow. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no external network/subprocess/API contract and no AI behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Build two real test plugins in a temp directory: A depends on B. Each `onLoad` appends its name to a shared log file/array. Run loader and assert observed order is `[B, A]`.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection outcome must propagate through dependency graph resolution into load suppression for both plugins, which is cross-module orchestration. Unit tests of graph logic alone can miss loader-state integration bugs. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: local dependency metadata only; no real external infra or AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B with mutual dependencies and `onLoad` hooks that emit observable side effects. Run loader and assert: (1) cycle error/report is emitted, and (2) neither plugin `onLoad` side effect occurs.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is specifically about sequencing between module import execution and lifecycle hook invocation, which requires loader + module system interaction. Unit-only tests of hook dispatcher miss import-time side effects/order bugs. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no subprocess/network/external API and no AI/model steps.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin entry module that sets a global/sentinel during module top-level execution; `onLoad` asserts that sentinel is already set and records success. Run loader and assert `onLoad` runs after import execution (assertion passes, order log confirms import side effect first).

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: This is resilience and dependency-aware continuation across multiple plugins (error handling + dependency graph + scheduler). Unit tests of try/catch logic alone can miss cross-plugin continuation/skip behavior. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no real external infra requirement, no AI output dependence, no multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Set up plugins: F throws in `onLoad`; C has no dependency on F; D depends on F. Run loader and assert C loads successfully, D is skipped/not initialized due to dependency, and error for F is reported without aborting whole initialization.
